content: Hey, I'm so excited for my upcoming trip to Costa Rica on August 15th. I've been practicing my Spanish and can't wait to try all the local cuisine, especially gallo pinto.
That sounds amazing! Costa Rica is a beautiful country. What made you decide to visit there?

I've always been fascinated by the rainforests and exotic wildlife. Plus, my colleague, Rachel, recommended it after her trip last year. She said the people are really friendly and the coffee is incredible.
Coffee is a great reason to visit anywhere! Have you planned out your itinerary or are you more of a spontaneous traveler?

I've booked a few tours, including a guided hike on August 18th and a visit to the Arenal Volcano on August 20th. But I also want to leave some room for exploration and maybe even try my hand at DJing at a local bar in Tamarindo.
That sounds like a great balance. DJing is a unique hobby, by the way. How did you get into it?

Thanks! I've been DJing for about 5 years now, and I love how it allows me to express my creativity and connect with people through music. I've even played a few sets at events in Phoenix, where I live.
Phoenix is a great city. What do you like to do in your free time when you're not working as a UX researcher or DJing?
I'm also really into tennis and archery. I try to play tennis at least once a week and practice archery on the weekends. It's a great way to clear my head and stay active.
It's great that you have so many hobbies. Do you find that they help you with your work in any way?

Definitely. My experience with archery has actually helped me with my focus and attention to detail, which is really valuable in my work as a UX researcher. And playing tennis has taught me a lot about teamwork and communication.
That's really interesting. It's great how you can apply the skills you learn from your hobbies to your work. Are you planning on trying any new activities or hobbies in Costa Rica?
I'd love to try surfing or paddleboarding while I'm there. And maybe even take a class on coffee brewing, since I've heard the coffee in Costa Rica is amazing.
Those sound like great ideas. You should definitely try to take a coffee brewing class. And don't forget to try some of the local fruits and dishes, like empanadas and ceviche.
Mmm, my mouth is watering just thinking about it. I'm so excited for this trip and can't wait to share all my experiences with my friends and family back in Phoenix when I return on September 1st.
